title: Executive Order 12610—Continuance of Certain Federal Advisory Committees

flags.justification: A routine, periodic continuation of eleven already-established advisory committees under standard Federal Advisory Committee Act renewal procedures.
Each continued committee retains its own underlying order's reporting requirements.
Bundling multiple unrelated committees into one periodic administrative renewal order is a standard housekeeping practice, not a forced acceptance of substantively unrelated policy provisions.
An administrative continuation and termination action, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Sec. 1 sets an explicit continuation end date: September 30, 1989.

Sec. 3 properly terminates eight commissions whose work is complete (including the Presidential Commission on the Space Shuttle Challenger Accident) rather than allowing them to linger indefinitely — a positive governance-hygiene action.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Routine, periodic administrative housekeeping with no novel authority claim.
Each committee remains under its own designated department.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. A genuinely well-designed periodic administrative cleanup: renewing still-active committees with a defined end date while properly closing out completed ones, including the Challenger Accident Commission.
